title: Climate Change and Environment
gist: This is a PIIE topic landing page indexing recent climate and environment research rather than a single argument. The listed items flag economically material threads: El Nino's multi-hundred-billion-to-trillion-dollar drag on global output, data centers pushing up utility costs, and the geopolitics of critical minerals.

insights.claim: An El Nino event could reduce global economic output by roughly $700 billion to $3 trillion, with the magnitude depending on policy choices rather than being fixed.
insights.speaker: Cullen S. Hendrix (PIIE)
insights.so_what: Sizes climate-driven macro downside that flows into commodity prices, insurance, and EM growth; watch agricultural and energy exposures during El Nino years.
insights.quote: El Nino could drag down the global economy by almost $700 billion or $3 trillion—and the choice is ours
insights.locator: RealTime Economics, El Nino could drag down

insights.claim: Climate inaction is being framed as an affordability problem, tying the cost of delayed climate policy directly to household living costs.
insights.speaker: Kimberly Clausing (PIIE)
insights.so_what: Reframing climate policy around affordability signals the political and fiscal framing likely to shape US regulation and green-subsidy debates.
insights.quote: Climate Inaction is an Affordability Problem
insights.locator: Testimony, Climate Inaction is an Affordability

insights.claim: There is an open policy question over whether data centers are driving up utility costs for other electricity consumers.
insights.speaker: Monica de Bolle (PIIE)
insights.so_what: Data-center load growth as a driver of retail utility rates is a live risk for utility regulation and a demand tailwind for power generation.
insights.quote: Are data centers driving up utility costs?
insights.locator: Policy for the Planet, Are data centers driving

insights.claim: Critical minerals are increasingly analyzed through a geopolitical lens rather than purely a market-supply one.
insights.speaker: Monica de Bolle (PIIE)
insights.so_what: Geopolitical framing of critical minerals points to supply-chain and trade-policy risk for battery, EV, and clean-energy inputs.
insights.locator: Policy for the Planet, The geopolitics behind critical
